pages.text: The $(item)Jet$() shoves a current through the water ahead of it — about 24 blocks. Riders in the current accelerate hard.$(br2)$(bold)Aim the nozzle into the water.$() A jet mounted above the waterline pushes nothing.
Spacing is your speed dial: sparse jets = lazy river, dense jets = screamer. A redstone signal switches a jet off.

Jets aim all six ways — $(bold)straight up included$(). An up-jet under a water column or tube shaft launches riders skyward, and a climb costs exactly what a drop pays, so a well-jetted slide can run uphill... or forever.
pages.title: Uphill & geysers
